channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [RPOWER] Reliance Power: 21.82 Cr Warrants Lapsed Due to Non-Conversion
item.description:
Reliance Power Ltd has announced that 21.82 crore outstanding warrants, previously issued by the company, have lapsed. This occurred because the warrant holders failed to convert them into equity shares within the stipulated 18-month period. Consequently, the amount initially paid on these warrants has also been forfeited. This update is in accordance with Regulation 30 of the Listing Regulations, 2015, and related SEBI Circulars. The lapsing of these warrants prevents potential equity dilution that would have occurred upon their conversion.

  • 21.82 crore Reliance Power warrants lapsed.
  • Warrants were not converted within 18 months.
  • Amount paid on lapsed warrants also forfeited.
  • Prevents potential equity dilution for shareholders.
  • Update aligns with Listing Regulations, 2015.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RELIANCE POWER LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [ROADSTAR] RIIT's ICRA A (Stable) Issuer Rating Revalidated
item.description:
Roadstar Infra Investment Trust (RIIT) announced that ICRA Limited has revalidated its Issuer Rating as [ICRA]A (Stable). This confirmation was communicated via a letter from ICRA dated April 27, 2026, in response to RIIT's request. The rating, which was originally assigned on May 29, 2025, continues to stand valid. This disclosure is made in accordance with Regulation 23 of the SEBI (Infrastructure Investment Trusts) Regulations, 2014, and relevant SEBI circulars. The revalidation letter is enclosed for reference, and the information is also available on the Trust's website.

  • ICRA revalidated RIIT's Issuer Rating as [ICRA]A (Stable).
  • The rating, originally from May 2025, remains valid.
  • This confirms the Trust's creditworthiness.
  • Disclosure complies with SEBI InvIT Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Roadstar Infra Investment Trust | Category: Rating

item.title: [FSL] Firstsource Solutions: Jagriti Bhattacharyya resigns as EVP & General Counsel
item.description:
Firstsource Solutions Ltd announced the resignation of Ms. Jagriti Bhattacharyya from her position as Executive Vice President - General Counsel and Senior Management Personnel. The company informed this development via a letter dated April 28, 2026, in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. Ms. Bhattacharyya's cessation from the role will be effective June 30, 2026.

  • Jagriti Bhattacharyya resigned from Firstsource Solutions Ltd.
  • She served as Executive Vice President - General Counsel.
  • Her resignation is effective from June 30, 2026.
  • The company disclosed this as per SEBI Listing Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: FIRSTSOURCE SOLUTIONS LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [AVG] AVG Logistics Board to meet May 4 for ₹53 Cr Rights Issue terms
item.description:
AVG Logistics Ltd. has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting for May 4, 2026. The primary agenda is to consider and approve various terms and conditions related to the previously approved Rights Issue. This includes determining the Issue price, Rights Entitlement ratio, and the Record Date. The company had approved the offer and issuance of fully paid-up equity shares via a Rights Issue for an amount not exceeding ₹5,300 Lakhs (₹53 crore) at its board meeting on April 28, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 4, 2026.
  • Meeting to finalize terms for the Rights Issue.
  • Rights Issue approved for up to ₹53 crore.
  • Key terms like issue price and record date to be decided.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: AVG Logistics Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [FIVESTAR] Five-Star Business Finance Board Amends Code of Fair Disclosure
item.description:
Five-Star Business Finance Ltd's Board of Directors, in a meeting held on April 28, 2026, approved amendments to the Code of Fair Disclosure. This action was taken pursuant to Regulation 8 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015, as amended. The updated Code is now accessible on the company's official website for investors.

  • Board approved amendments to Code of Fair Disclosure.
  • Action taken under SEBI (Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015.
  • Updated code available on company's investor website.
  • Board meeting held on Tuesday, April 28, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Five-Star Business Finance Ltd | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [LOTUSDEV] Sri Lotus Developers receives ₹10 Cr GST Show Cause Notice
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Ltd announced that its merged entity, Veer Savarkar Projects Private Limited, has received a Show Cause Notice (Form GST DRC-01) from the Assistant Commissioner of State Tax, Mumbai. The notice, dated April 28, 2026, pertains to alleged GST violations for FY 2020-21, 2021-22, and 2023-24, involving a total alleged liability of approximately ₹10.00 crore, including tax, interest, and penalty. The company has already paid the tax component under protest and plans to submit an appropriate response contesting the allegations, stating there will be no material impact on its financials or operations.

  • Merged entity received a GST Show Cause Notice for FY20-21, FY21-22, FY23-24.
  • Alleged liability totals approximately ₹10.00 crore (tax, interest, penalty).
  • Company has paid the tax component under protest.
  • Management believes there will be no material financial or operational impact.
  • Company plans to contest the allegations with a formal response.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sri Lotus Developers and Realty Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [PICCADIL] Piccadily Agro FY26 Revenue Crosses ₹1,100 Cr, Alco-bev Up 42%
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Piccadily Agro Industries Limited reported strong financial results for FY26, achieving a record total revenue of ₹1,143 crore, surpassing the ₹1,100 crore milestone. This growth was primarily driven by the aggressive expansion of its distillery business and premium spirit offerings. The Alco-bev division's revenue surged 42.1% year-over-year to ₹908 crore. The company's Profit Before Tax (PBT) increased by 33% to ₹192 crore, with Net Profit reaching ₹140 crore. In Q4 FY26, the Alco-bev division saw revenue jump 67% YoY to ₹250 crore and PBT rise 79% to ₹63 crore. The company's performance was bolstered by strong consumer demand for its premium brands. Piccadily Agro is also undergoing a strategic demerger of its sugar division, aiming to become a pure-play alco-beverage company by the end of FY27, enhancing strategic focus and value creation. Additionally, its Chhattisgarh facility is set to begin monetization from May 2026.

  • FY26 total revenue reached a record ₹1,143 crore, up 28% YoY.
  • Alco-bev division revenue surged 42.1% YoY to ₹908 crore.
  • PBT grew 33% to ₹192 crore; Net Profit reached ₹140 crore.
  • Q4 Alco-bev revenue rose 67% YoY; PBT increased 79% YoY.
  • Company plans demerger of sugar division to focus on alco-bev.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PICCADILY AGRO INDUSTRIES LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [PPLPHARMA] Piramal Pharma Board Approves FY26 Results; Re-appoints 4 Directors
item.description:
Piramal Pharma's Board of Directors, at its meeting on April 28, 2026, approved the Audited Financial Statements and Results (Standalone & Consolidated) for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Board also approved the re-appointment of four key directors, subject to shareholder approval. Ms. Nandini Piramal was re-appointed as Whole-Time Director, Executive Director, and Chairperson for three years from April 1, 2027. Mr. Peter DeYoung was re-appointed as Whole-Time Director and Executive Director for three years from October 6, 2026. Mr. Sridhar Gorthi and Mr. Peter Stevenson were both re-appointed as Non-Executive, Independent Directors for a second term of five years from March 30, 2027, with Mr. Stevenson continuing post 75 years of age.

  • Board approved Audited Financial Results for Q4 and financial year ended March 31, 2026.
  • Ms. Nandini Piramal re-appointed as Whole-Time Director & Chairperson for 3 years.
  • Mr. Peter DeYoung re-appointed as Whole-Time Director for 3 years.
  • Mr. Sridhar Gorthi and Mr. Peter Stevenson re-appointed as Independent Directors for 5 years.
  • All director re-appointments are subject to shareholder approval.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Piramal Pharma Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [PPLPHARMA] Piramal Pharma Board Approves FY26 Results; Re-appoints 4 Directors
item.description:
Piramal Pharma Ltd's Board of Directors, at its meeting on April 28, 2026, approved the Audited Financial Statements and Results (Standalone & Consolidated) for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Board also approved the re-appointment of Ms. Nandini Piramal as Whole-Time Director and Chairperson for three years from April 1, 2027. Mr. Peter DeYoung was re-appointed as Whole-Time Director for three years from October 6, 2026. Additionally, Mr. Sridhar Gorthi and Mr. Peter Stevenson were re-appointed as Non-Executive, Independent Directors for second terms of five years each, effective March 30, 2027. All re-appointments are subject to shareholder approval.

  • Board approved audited financial results for Q4 and FY26.
  • Ms. Nandini Piramal re-appointed as Executive Director & Chairperson.
  • Mr. Peter DeYoung re-appointed as Executive Director.
  • Two Independent Directors, Mr. Sridhar Gorthi and Mr. Peter Stevenson, re-appointed.
  • All director re-appointments are subject to shareholder approval.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Piramal Pharma Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [MITSHI] Mitshi India Q4/FY26 Results Approved; Auditor Issues Qualified Opinion
item.description:
Mitshi India's Board of Directors approved the standalone audited financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. However, the independent auditor, SDPM & Co, issued a qualified opinion on these results. The qualification stems from the company's substantial cash transactions in its agriculture product trading operations, where the auditor relied on GST returns for turnover due to non-banking sales. Additionally, outstanding balances of trade receivables, trade payables, and unsecured loans are subject to confirmation and supporting documents, impacting the true and fair view of the financial information.

  • Board approved standalone audited financial results for Q4 and FY26.
  • Auditor issued a qualified opinion on the financial results.
  • Qualification due to substantial cash transactions in agriculture trading.
  • Auditor relied on GST returns for turnover due to non-banking sales.
  • Outstanding balances of receivables/payables require confirmation.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mitshi India Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result
